name: Layout Intent
summary: Protocol defining the application shell structure and region mechanics (Drawers, Rails, Panels).

behavior.description: How regions interact with the main content area

variant.description: Purely-presentational treatment of a region's surface, orthogonal to `behavior` and behavior-free (open-numbered-variants, platform-decisions.md#open-numbered-variants — generalized from Action #1318 to layout per #1322/#1323). The listed values are the recommended core set, not a closed enum: an open-numbered axis, so authors mint additional members per the variant contract (a value names a presentational treatment that leaves semantics and behavior unchanged). Optional with a most-permissive default: absent → the conventional surface for the region. Surface is a plain attribute consumed by CSS (`[pane][variant]`); no wrapper required. Structural differences (a rail vs an overlay) are `behavior`/block polymorphism, never variants.

description:
Region Behavior

  • push: Region consumes space, shifting the main content (Desktop).
  • overlay: Region floats above content with a backdrop (Mobile).
  • rail: Region collapses to a thin strip (Icons) rather than disappearing.

Anatomy

  • shell: The global viewport container.
  • pane: A resizable subdivision of the shell.
  • dock: A sticky region (Header/Footer).
